acceptance:
## Summary
`GET /agents/{agent_id}/status` currently has no auth dependency, which means anyone with a UUID can read agent status, heartbeat timing, and uptime data.

## Why This Matters
- runtime/status endpoints should follow the same ownership and auth story as the rest of the control plane
- exposing agent health by UUID is an avoidable information leak
- the endpoint is also lightly documented and lightly tested, so regressions are easy to miss

## Evidence
- `src/api/routes/agent_runtime.py:358`
- `docs/api/index.md:19`
- `docs/api/agents.md:16`
- `tests/api/test_ingest.py:64`

## Acceptance Criteria
- decide whether the endpoint should remain public, require agent auth, or require user auth plus ownership checks
- implement the chosen auth policy consistently
- document the endpoint and its intended audience clearly
- add auth/ownership tests and one happy-path test for the allowed caller

## Notes
This may touch adjacent auth-hardening work, but it looks like a specific endpoint-level gap that deserves explicit tracking.
title: Lock down or remove unauthenticated agent status lookup
